message: Jake
Meryl
Hey there.
Front Desk
Welcome. Miss Kline is out.
I see.
You don't know when she'll be back, do you?
Front Desk
I'm sorry.
I'll come back later.
Front Desk
She hasn't returned yet.
I see.
I wonder where she went.
Front Desk
Welcome, are you here for Miss Kline?
Yeah, that's right.
Is she in her room?
Front Desk
No, she is currently unavailable.
Oh, she's not here? Do you know when she left?
Front Desk
Well...
I’m not sure. I can’t be of much help.
I see.
So she isn't here.
Front Desk
She hasn't returned yet.
I see.
I wonder where she went.
Oh, long time no see!
It sounds like you've had a lot going on, I've heard from Martin.
Oh, welcome.

From the window, you should be able to overlook one of the most glamorous streets in Manhattan, but the curtains block the view.
On the large leather sofa,
someone is sprawled out. The smell of alcohol seems to be coming from
their body.
On the table, there are remnants of room service food and a nearly empty bottle. I wonder when they started drinking.
They don't seem that drunk, but it looks like they can't stop relying on alcohol.
Compared to the worst times, things seem to have improved somewhat. Their complexion isn't bad, and their behavior is within the realm of normal.
They're dressed just like usual, with a thin gown over their bare skin.
Is this normal for them?
Have you been away from New York for a while?
Yeah, just a little getaway for some fresh air. I still can't believe about Ariana.
I was relieved to see that you look well.
What? Did you think I was going to commit suicide or something?
No way, not at all.
I can't just keep feeling down forever.
By the way, you must have gone through a lot, right? I heard you were taken in by the police.
Actually, I was suspected of being the perpetrator in a serial murder case.
Oh really?
You were the culprit?
N-No, that's not true!
Right?
Innocent until proven guilty, after all.
Cheer up! Want to have a drink?
That guy was the culprit. I bet he committed suicide to atone for his sins.
I believe he's not the perpetrator.
That's fine, you were friends after all.
I'm actually relieved that guy is dead. Is it bad to say that? But that's the honest truth.
If Gai wasn't the perpetrator, would you take back what you just said? As a friend, I don’t want to believe he could be the one.
Then bring in the real culprit! If you do, I'll stop saying bad things.
Prove Gai's innocence.
I had many discussions about that kid with Gloria. It's too late now, but I wonder why I acted like I wanted to let him go back then.
Hehe, it's too late to regret, though.
I should have told you sooner that Cindy is your daughter.
It's okay. I wonder how I would have reacted if I found out right away.
At least I didn't think well of Cindy.
But it's strange. Even though I talked to that kid several times, I never realized she was my daughter. I couldn't say I'm her mother.
It's understandable after being apart for 16 years.
Hmm, I feel like I should have sensed something if it were true. Yet I didn't feel anything.
I couldn't feel anything.
I wanted to be called her mother. That's my only regret, I wonder.
It seems like Martin has something bothering him. Is it about Patricia?
Apparently, it seems so.
That father and daughter are too close, and they fight a lot. They probably interfere with each other way too much.
It's over between him and me.
After the party, I got really angry, and since then, we've been in touch, but we can’t go back.
Still, doesn't it mean it’s not decided yet?
It's fine, there's no need for comfort.
It's really okay, I've come to genuinely feel that way, not just out of stubbornness.
It's really over between Martin and me.
In the end, is it decided that Ariana's death was the work of that culprit?
It seems that's how the police see it, but apparently, there hadn't been a pattern of crimes committed in the same place before.
I really want them to catch the culprit soon.
I can't escape from New York until I see the culprit's face. I really don't want to be in a place like this.
Do you really hate the culprit that much?
Of course.
That's right. But I've been getting calmer lately. Rather than hatred, I feel like this is my duty.
Cindy.
Do you feel guilty about Ariana being killed?
That's right.
I really want them to catch the culprit.
A little trip, huh?
I wonder if it's like a heartbroken journey after losing my daughter.
Why would I know that I was taken to the police?
It's reasonable to think I heard it from the director.
I'm calmer than I imagined. Have I sorted out my feelings?
Guy died leaving a mystery behind.
It seems like she feels somewhat relieved due to believing that Guy is the culprit.
Could it really be that Guy killed Cindy? No way, I can’t believe such a thing.
I find it hard to believe it was a suicide.
Before Cindy died,
the only regret I have is that I couldn’t tell her that she was my mother.
If Cindy were alive,
would they have become a close mother-daughter pair, or would they have been at odds?
It was a well-known fact about the director and
the atmosphere suggests that Jennifer had taken her place when thinking about the party.
Is her relationship still strained with her daughter Patricia?
It’s still unclear if Cindy and Eva's cases are one of the serial murder cases.
Simply considering the method, it seems likely they could be the work of the same perpetrator,
but to be mistaken for the culprit myself is absurd.
Is the police just randomly suspecting related individuals without any real evidence?
It’s still hard to say it’s completely resolved.
※※※ It’s a bug ※※※
